description_long: This object is a partially damaged or deteriorated airplane with a largely exposed and incomplete fuselage. The structure features two prominent, jagged side walls that suggest the remnants of a cockpit and passenger cabin, with the central section open and missing its roof. The wings are either missing or heavily fragmented, and the tail section is not clearly defined, indicating significant structural loss. The surface appears to be composed of corrugated metal with a weathered, grayish finish, and there are scattered debris-like elements nearby. The overall form is skeletal, with visible gaps and torn edges, giving the impression of an abandoned or wrecked aircraft.
description: A heavily damaged, skeletal airplane with exposed fuselage and missing wings, featuring weathered metal surfaces and jagged edges.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the jagged, torn fuselage walls and a partially intact floor, with some debris visible to the left.
description_view_1: The right side view shows the open central section and the fragmented side walls, with a clearer view of the missing tail and some debris to the right.
description_view_2: The rear view emphasizes the open, hollow fuselage and the absence of a tail section, with the floor structure more visible.
description_view_3: The front view displays the open nose area and the incomplete, sloped fuselage sides, with the interior exposed.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, fragmented wings; no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles due to damage.
attribute_tail_design: No discernible tail structure; tail section appears missing or destroyed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted cockpit, open and exposed due to missing canopy and structural damage.
